note: First calibration block. The four corridors ranked highest by ascertainment-adjusted 30-day export risk in the 20 May 2026 PHEIC-era snapshot, pinned as the method's pre-committed uncertainty test. Carried forward unchanged on every later refresh; scored against public reports on 2026-06-19.

Second calibration block, pinned before publication of the 21 May 2026 snapshot. This block augments the initial top-four block with a designed sample across the current watchlist's relative high/mid/low bands, cross-border and in-country corridors, and likely positive / likely negative controls. Because the aggregate-input model still clusters all 18 watchlist corridors in a narrow probability band, these are relative tiers within the 21 May watchlist, not absolute low/medium/high probabilities.

Third calibration block. Graduates Goma (goma-cod) from geographic-anchor-not-in-calibration to a pinned candidate target on 2026-05-26 per founder direction (see .process/2026-05-26-pre-refresh-decisions/follow-up-after-pushback.md, Thread 2 path A). Pins the three Ituri source HZs -> goma-cod corridors at the ascertainment-adjusted 50% intervals from the 2026-05-26 pipeline run with goma-cod added to candidate_target_zones. Pre-pin prior context (NOT a resolution event): one laboratory-confirmed BDBV case in Goma on 2026-05-17, a woman who travelled from Ituri Province (per data/zones.json:goma-cod and data/external_sources/bdbv-2026.observed.json). Doctrine[4] early-lock will fire only on a new lab-confirmed case landing in Goma strictly inside [2026-05-26, 2026-06-25T23:59:59Z]; the 17-May case is outside that window and is therefore not a doctrine[4] trigger. Carried forward unchanged on every later refresh; scored against public reports on 2026-06-25.

Fourth calibration block. Pins the 2026-06-04 west/SSD corridor expansion: two new candidate targets (yei-ssd, kisangani-cod) graduate to pinned corridor endpoints, and the four highest-signal source-to-target corridors to them are committed at the ascertainment-adjusted 50% intervals from the 2026-06-04 pipeline run with both targets added to candidate_target_zones. yei-ssd tests northward cross-border exportation to South Sudan (West Nile / Morobo-Kaya axis); kisangani-cod tests the westward Tshopo / Congo-river axis (RN4 via Mambasa and Bafwasende), a no-PCR surveillance gap. The aru -> yei-ssd point is a designed geography-vs-load blindspot probe (frontier geography flags Aru while the case-load model rates it low). Corridor basis: WHO DON603 (2026-05-29). Carried forward unchanged on every later refresh; scored against public reports on 2026-07-04.
